name: Shona Sculpture

visual_attributes.morphology: abstract and figurative forms often depicting human and animal figures
distinctive_features: use of serpentine stone, particularly springstone
smooth, polished surfaces with intricate details
often features elongated forms and stylized figures
visual_attributes.texture: smooth and polished with occasional rough areas for contrast
visual_attributes.size_and_scale: varies from small handheld pieces to large monumental works
visual_attributes.structural_arrangement: balanced compositions with a focus on symmetry and flow

domain_specific_visual.cultural_motifs: incorporates Shona cultural symbols and spiritual themes

domain_specific_functional.economic_role: source of income for artists and communities

relationships.description: Shona sculpture is crafted by artists from the Shona ethnic group in Zimbabwe.
Shona sculptures are frequently displayed at the National Gallery of Zimbabwe.

contextual_attributes.cultural_significance: represents the artistic and spiritual heritage of the Shona people

contrastive_constraints: NOT made from wood or metal
